title: thymus ciliatus var. sublobatus
intro: Thymus ciliatus var. sublobatus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe, characterized by its small, pinkish-white flowers and needle-like leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe minor wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Consuming creeping thyme may also offer various health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced stress, and enhanced immune function, due to its high content of antioxidants and essential oils. Creeping thyme can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, essential oils, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health concerns.
